basics.name: Joe Pegler
basics.label: Senior Full-Stack Engineer · Product, Platforms, and Developer Tooling
basics.summary: Senior full-stack engineer who ships product and platform systems end to end across React, Node.js, SDKs, and infrastructure. I specialize in high-reliability execution and developer tooling, with deep Web3 experience in smart accounts, simulation, and transaction pipelines.

selectedImpact: Built and open-sourced Statecraft, a TypeScript SDK for deterministic Ethereum integration testing with typed fixtures, pinned state, and isolated runtimes.
Designed and shipped an internal transaction bundler with retries, simulation, and structured error decoding to improve delivery reliability for sponsored and gasless flows.
Built a unified quote and route orchestration layer across EVM and Solana providers, replacing chain-specific integrations with a single execution surface.
Owned the core TypeScript SDK for smart-account, quote, and execution flows, reducing integration complexity for frontend and backend product teams.
Built Biconomy's flagship TypeScript SDK from scratch and led architecture and API design for smart-account and multi-chain execution integrations.
Implemented EIP-712 and EIP-1271 signing with P-256 passkey support to enable keyless and gasless transaction UX.
Led end-to-end delivery of Enso's primary dApp and execution stack, spanning frontend product work, backend services, and on-chain data pipelines.
Built automated CI/CD for CoinFLEX's trading interface and led frontend standards at GameAnalytics, improving release safety and team delivery quality.

work.name: defi.app
work.summary: Own transaction infrastructure and developer tooling for a unified cross-chain DeFi product.
highlights: Designed and shipped an internal transaction bundler with retries, simulation, and ABI-backed error decoding to improve reliability in product-facing execution flows.
Built and open-sourced Statecraft to make Ethereum integration tests deterministic through typed fixtures, pinned state, and per-file isolated runtimes.
Built cross-chain quote and route orchestration across LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, Mayan, LayerZero, and Relay behind one execution surface.
Owned the TypeScript SDK (RouteClient and ExecutionManager) for smart-account, quote, and execution paths used by frontend and backend teams.
Delivered EVM and Solana simulation plus sponsorship infrastructure, including Jito-aware validation for sponsored transaction safety.

work.name: biconomy
work.summary: Owned the core developer SDK for smart accounts and multi-chain execution.
highlights: Built the flagship TypeScript SDK from scratch and led architecture and API design for smart-account and multi-chain execution.
Implemented EIP-712 and EIP-1271 signing with P-256 passkey support to enable keyless and gasless UX patterns.
Shipped viem-based modular clients with Safe support, reducing complexity for account and bundler integrations.
Hardened production readiness with typed errors, composable batching, and CI coverage using Vitest, Bun, GitHub Actions, and Codecov.

work.name: enso
work.summary: Led 0 to 1 delivery of Enso's client dApp and execution stack.
highlights: Owned end-to-end delivery of Enso's primary Ethereum dApp, from frontend experience to backend execution services.
Built and maintained the Graph-based indexing pipeline used by both product and backend systems for on-chain data.
Shipped and extended intents and solver infrastructure for decentralized trade execution.

work.name: coinflex
work.summary: Led the trading interface and deployment system for the live exchange product.
highlights: Led delivery of the platform's trading UI, owning frontend architecture, core feature rollout, and engineering standards.
Built automated CI/CD for the interface stack, reducing release risk and increasing shipping velocity on a live trading product.

earlierExperience: GameAnalytics - Lead Frontend Developer (2016 to 2019): led a team of five, defined frontend architecture and quality standards, and won company MVP 2018/19.
FSI, Healthcare 21, and Nixatel (2013 to 2016): built cross-platform enterprise applications and delivered 0 to 1 mobile and web products in small teams.
